title: The Best of Both Worlds
concept: Hybrid architectures combine the local extraction efficiency of CNNs with the global context modeling of Transformers, addressing the limitations of both.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why are CNNs generally better than ViTs on tiny datasets?

options.text: They have strong built-in visual inductive biases like locality.
They do not use gradient descent.

exercises.explain: Correct! CNNs assume pixels are spatially related, which acts as a guide rail when data is scarce.

cards.title: A new species
cards.body: CNNs are incredibly efficient at capturing local details but struggle with global context. Transformers are masters of global relationships but take massive data to learn basic geometry. What if we cross-bred them to create a new, high-performance hybrid species?

cards.title: Hybrid Vision Models
cards.body: A Hybrid CNN-Transformer is an architecture that integrates convolutional layers and self-attention blocks. Typically, convolutions extract local features (edges, textures) in the early stages, while transformers process these features globally in the later stages.

cards.title: A chef's prep cook
cards.body: Imagine a kitchen. A master chef (Transformer) is great at planning complex recipes and balancing flavors, but they shouldn't waste time chopping 50 onions. We hire a prep cook (CNN) to chop the ingredients (extract local features) and pass them in neat bowls to the chef, who creates the final masterpiece.

cards.title: Faster training
cards.body: By adding just 4 convolutional layers at the very start of a Vision Transformer, researchers found the model could train on standard ImageNet-1k without overfitting, matching the training speed and stability of standard CNNs.

cards.title: Not just patching them together
cards.body: You might think a hybrid model is just a ResNet glued to a ViT. While that is one way (backbone hybrid), modern hybrids are designed from scratch, interleaving convolutions and attention layers to optimize speed and parameters.

cards.title: Pure ViT vs. Hybrid Model
cards.body: Comparing early stage processing in vision networks:

left.text:
Choops image into raw patches
Linear projection only
Needs millions of training images

right.text:
Convolutional feature extractor
Extracts rich edge features first
Trains stably on smaller datasets

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of a Hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture?
exercises.explain: Hybrid networks leverage the strengths of both architectures, using convs for local textures and attention for global layout.

options.text: To combine CNN's local feature efficiency with Transformer's global modeling power
To double the size of the input image before training
To run models exclusively on CPUs to save energy
To replace all activation functions with pooling operations

exercises.prompt: True or false: Hybrid models generally require less training data to converge than pure Vision Transformers.
exercises.explain: True. The convolutional components inject local spatial bias, helping the model learn geometric patterns with far less data.
Self-attention serves as the global modeling mechanism in hybrid vision models.
exercises.sentence: Hybrid architectures combine convolutions for local features and ___ for global relationships.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes a hybrid vision model's early processing pipeline?
exercises.explain: Hybrids often run convolutional layers first to extract feature maps, which are then reshaped into tokens for the transformer.

options.text: Running the image through several convolutional blocks to get a feature map, then feeding that map into a transformer
Running self-attention on raw pixels, then averaging them with a Sobel filter
Flattening the image immediately and passing it straight to an MLP

exercises.prompt: How does having a convolutional 'prep stage' help the transformer layer that follows it?

options.text: It provides rich, pre-filtered local feature tokens, freeing the transformer from having to learn basic edge detection from scratch.
It increases the sequence length by ten times.
It removes all position embeddings from the sequence.
It forces the attention weights to be uniform.

exercises.explain: Magnificent! Convolutions feed clean feature maps to the transformer, allowing it to focus on high-level global context immediately. Next: using a CNN backbone as a feature extractor!
